planning_review.notes: The large-file queue had only test/frontend-acceptance.test.js remaining. Codex selected the resource-readiness cluster because the tests are independent artifact validation cases with narrow shared fixture imports and no implementation changes.

moved_tests: frontend acceptance artifact fails closed on blocking findings
frontend acceptance artifact requires desktop and mobile viewport evidence
frontend acceptance requires mounted route and explicit workbench favicon readiness
frontend acceptance blocks root favicon fallback links
frontend acceptance accepts mounted Next workbench favicon links
frontend acceptance requires mounted SVG favicon MIME evidence

extracted_modules.domain: artifact false-pass validation, required viewport evidence, mounted route and favicon readiness, root favicon fallback rejection, mounted Next favicon acceptance, and mounted SVG MIME drift

post_extraction_review.notes: Read-only bounded review found no duplicated or missing test names, no manifest completion mislabel, and no assertion or test-coverage weakening in the extracted resource-readiness shard.

delta_review.notes: Read-only delta review found the legacy-static inventory update to be a reasonable follow-up to the split, with no hidden legacy static coverage gap.

verification_notes: Initial target checks passed for node --check, focused frontend acceptance tests, and npm run check:large-files.
The first final npm test rerun passed; the first closeout rerun failed one workbench-server-shard-03 scheduler-dispatch assertion with 400 instead of 201.
The failed shard was rerun directly and passed.
A second npm run check:closeout passed, so the first closeout failure was treated as transient and not as a P36 test split regression.
